title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves comparable quality to much larger competitors while running on a single GPU. The model supports deployment on mobile devices (Pixel phones with E2B and E4B variants), medical applications on two GPUs for hospital systems, and multi-agent workloads on consumer hardware like M4 Macs via LM Studio. The license transition from custom Gemma to Apache 2.0 simplifies procurement for sovereign institutions.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma 4, focusing on open-model ownership for sovereign institutions. The 31B model reaches quality comparable to much larger competitors while running on a single GPU, with deployments spanning Pixel phones (E2B and E4B variants), hospital medical systems on two GPUs, and multi-agent workloads on consumer hardware such as M4 Macs via LM Studio. The license shift from the custom Gemma terms to Apache 2.0 simplifies procurement. The throughline: comparable capability at smaller scale, broad deployment targets, and a permissive license aimed at institutions seeking control over their models.
